quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g. West 'roughly 35% below North', Gadget '$2,392.00 from only 16 units, reflecting its high unit price of $149.50', and mean $238.48 vs median $204.94.

specificity.why: Each recommendation names concrete products/regions and data-grounded logic, e.g. 'Bundle low-priced Sprockets with Widgets or Gadgets' and 'Investigate West's underperformance versus North.'

insight_density.why: It surfaces several non-obvious observations, including the mean/median skew ('a few larger purchases pulling the mean upward'), Sprocket's volume-vs-price inversion (238 units yet lowest revenue at $4.75), and regional concentration ('no single region exceeding a third of the total').
